               S U P R E M E  C O U R T  O F  I N D I A
                       RECORD OF PROCEEDINGS
PETITION(S) FOR SPECIAL LEAVE TO APPEAL (C)  NO(S).  13127/2024
[Arising out of impugned final judgment and order dated  31-03-2023
in WP(C) No. 7180/2016 passed by the High Court of Orissa at
Cuttack]
THE MEMBER SECRETARY-CUM DIRECTOR, 
SWASTHYA SIKHYA   PETITIONER(S)
                                VERSUS
KISHOR MALLICK & ORS.                              RESPONDENT(S)
 
Date : 17-07-2026 This petition was called on for hearing today.
CORAM :  HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE SATISH CHANDRA SHARMA
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE NONGMEIKAPAM KOTISWAR SINGH
For Petitioner(s) :Mr. P. V. Yogeswaran, AOR
                   Ms. Shailja Singh, Adv.
                                      
For Respondent(s) : Mr. Manav Sabharwal, Adv. 
Ms. Shivangi Gupta, Adv. 
Mr. Shibashish Misra, AOR
              
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. This Court on 22.04.2024 has issued limited notice only on the
point of backwages, returnable in eight weeks. In spite of the fact
that no order was passed staying the reinstatement, the respondents
were not reinstated for the reasons best shown to the petitioner.
2.  On 25.03.2026, it was brought to the notice of this Court
that the workmen have not been reinstated and the learned counsel
for the petitioner took two weeks time to do the needful i.e. to
reinstate the workmen. Unfortunately, the workmen have not been
reinstated so far. This Court has carefully gone through the
judgment and is of the opinion that in the facts and circumstances
of the case backwages were rightly granted to workmen in question

--- Page 900 ---
2
and, therefore, no case for interference is made out in the matter.
Hence, the special leave petition is dismissed.
2.  Pending application(s), if any, shall stand disposed of. 
(JAGDISH KUMAR)                                 (KOMAL)
ASTT. REGISTRAR-cum-PS                        COURT MASTER (NSH)

        UPON hearing the counsel, the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
Though an application has been filed by one N. Ethiraju,
claiming to be a legatee under the Will said to have been executed
1

--- Page 914 ---
by late K.S. Susheelamma, we find that the said Will has not been
proved in accordance with law. As matters stand, all the legal
heirs of late K.S. Susheelamma are entitled to be brought on
record. 
Learned counsel appearing for N. Ethiraju shall file an
appropriate  application  to  implead  Swarnalatha  and  Asha,  the
daughters of K.S. Susheelamma, who were arrayed as defendant Nos. 1
and 3 in the suit, along with E. Rashmi and E. Ashwin, the grand-
children of K.S. Susheelamma, through her pre-deceased daughter,
Roopa, the wife of N. Ethiraju. 
The application in this regard shall be filed within two weeks
from today with full particulars to enable this Court to put the
aforestated persons on notice as to the pendency of this special
leave petition. 
Re-list on 13.08.2026.
(BABITA PANDEY)                              (PREETI SAXENA)

O R D E R
1. Counsel for the petitioners, at the outset, pointed out that
Ms. Tanveer Ara, w/o Abdul Wakeel Ansari, is required to join along
with the petitioners.
2. Since there is no conflict of interest between her and the
other petitioners, the prayer is allowed and Ms. Tanveer Ara is
added as co-petitioner. Cause title shall be accordingly amended.
3. Heard learned counsel for the petitioners.
4. The instant arbitration petition is allowed. Justice Asha
Menon, former Judge of the Delhi High Court, is appointed as sole
arbitrator.   All  the  contentions  including  arbitrability  and
maintainability sought to be raised on behalf of the contesting
respondents as well as on behalf of the petitioners are permitted
to be raised before the learned Arbitrator. It is clarified that we
have not expressed any opinion on merits of the case.

IA No. 196460/2026 - SETTING ASIDE AN ABATEMENT
 
Date : 22-07-2026 This matter was called on for hearing today.
CORAM : 
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE UJJAL BHUYAN
         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE ATUL S. CHANDURKAR
For Petitioner(s) :  Mr. Sukumar Pattjoshi, Sr. Adv.
 Mr. Jaydip Pati, AOR
 Mr. Prashant Katara, Adv.
 Mr. Sanjay Kumar Jha, Adv.
 Mr. Raina Anand, Adv.
 Ms. Sanjivani Pattjoshi, Adv. (not legible)
 Mr. Soin Khan, Adv.
 Mr. Sachin Mehta, Adv.
 Mr. Jaydip Pati, AOR (not legible)
                   
For Respondent(s) : 
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R
1. IA No. 196462/2026 seeking condonation of delay in filing the
applications for setting aside abatement; IA No. 196460/2026 for
1

--- Page 1054 ---
setting aside abatement and IA No. 196455/2026 for substitution,
are allowed.
2. We have heard Mr. Sukumar Pattjoshi, learned senior counsel
for the petitioner.
3. Permission to file Special Leave Petition is granted.
4. Delay in filing and refiling the Special Leave Petition
condoned.
5. Petitioner is aggrieved by the impugned order of the High
Court dated 01.10.2025, whereby the High Court has condoned the
delay of 2093 days in filing the application for restoration of
First Appeal No.331 of 1998.
6. On going through the said order, we find that High Court has
considered all aspects of the matter whereafter the delay has been
condoned. However, the High Court is mindful in directing that
since  the  appeal  has  been  restored,  the  appellant
(respondent/herein) should remove the office objections within a
period of 8 weeks from the date of the order, failing which the
appeal would stand dismissed.
7. In such circumstances, and more particularly, considering the
endeavour of the High Court to see that the first appeal is heard
on merits, we are not inclined to interfere with the impugned order
dated 01.10.2025.
8. Special Leave petition is accordingly dismissed.
9. Pending application(s), if any, shall stand disposed of.
  (IRAMNAZ)                                  (CHETNA BALOONI)
COURT MASTER (SH)                          COURT MASTER (NSH)
2

         HON'BLE MR. JUSTICE SHEEL NAGU

For Respondent(s) : 
          UPON hearing the counsel the Court made the following
                             O R D E R

liberty to the petitioner to file bail application afresh. As
liberty was already granted by the High Court to file a bail
petition, in case the trail concluded within a period of six
months.
2. Pending application(s), if any, shall stand disposed of. 
(JAGDISH KUMAR)                                 (KOMAL)
